authors: Sinclair NC, McDermott HJ, Bulluss KJ, Fallon JB, Perera T, Xu SS, Brown P, Thevathasan W
title: Subthalamic Nucleus Deep Brain Stimulation Evokes Resonant Neural Activity
study_question: Does deep brain stimulation evoke a resonant neural response that can serve as a biomarker?
population_assessed: 14 Parkinson disease (PD) and 5 essential tremor (ET) patients undergoing DBS implantation.
follow_up_duration: At least 3 months postsurgery (mean 390 days); one patient 560 days.
outcome_measures: Evoked resonant neural activity (ERNA) amplitude, UPDRS motor improvement.
inclusion_criteria: Patients undergoing awake deep brain stimulation implantation surgery for PD or ET.
answer_to_study_question: DBS evokes large-amplitude resonant neural activity (ERNA) in STN, correlating with therapeutic benefit.
